name: Alight, Inc.

profile.description: Alight, Inc. is a technology-enabled services company providing cloud-based human capital management solutions worldwide. Its Alight Worklife platform delivers integrated benefits administration, healthcare navigation, financial wellbeing, leave of absence management, and retiree healthcare services. The company operates AI-led capabilities through its LumenAI engine, serving employers with full-service customer care for health, wealth, and wellbeing lifecycle management. New CEO Rohit Verma joined in January 2026 to lead a turnaround.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Alight divested its payroll and professional services business in 2022 to focus on its cloud-based benefits platform, a modest strategic narrowing rather than a transformative pivot. Revenue has declined for two consecutive years, falling 3% in 2025 and -2.6% YoY in Q1 2026 [1]. AI Deployment (2/2): Alight launched LumenAI, its next-generation AI engine powering the Worklife platform, and the Alight Assistant — an AI-powered benefits navigator leveraging employer-specific plan documents for personalized enrollment guidance. Fortune named Alight one of America's Most Innovative Companies in 2026 [2]. Market Share (1/2): Alight serves ~70% of Fortune 100 employers in benefits administration, a meaningful niche position, but competes against scaled giants like ADP and Workday and is losing share with revenue declining and renewal cohorts shrinking [2]. Product Pipeline (0/2): No specific next-generation products with named launch dates beyond incremental LumenAI enhancements. The $100M+ 'knowledge layer' investment in 2026 is infrastructure, not expected to yield efficiencies until 2027 [2]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Technology investment through LumenAI and AI-powered platform capabilities is above average for HR services peers, consistent with the company's pivot toward AI-enabled service delivery [2].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Alight's stated purpose is 'Be a beacon. Empower others. Be Alight.' — clear in its intent to empower employees through benefits technology, but not distinctive enough to differentiate from any HR services peer. It identifies what the company does but doesn't articulate a deeper transformative vision [3]. Community Impact (1/2): Alight published a 2024 Global Impact Report, signed the Science Based Targets initiative commitment letter, and set a goal for 100% renewable electricity by 2032. General corporate responsibility programs exist but lack transformative community investment with specific dollar amounts [3]. Employee Retention (0/2): Glassdoor reviews reveal significant cultural challenges — employees describe invisible C-suite leadership, limited growth opportunities, inconsistent communication, and rare compensation increases. Executive team rated 'C-' on Comparably (bottom 30%). Three CEOs in recent years undermines organizational stability [2]. Insider Buying (1/2): Director Robert Lopes purchased $24,600 in shares at $0.82 in March 2026, and Director Russell Fradin received a $50,000 stock award — modest signals of insider conviction at depressed prices, though most insider activity is stock awards rather than open-market purchases [4].

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Alight's Infrastructure reflects severe financial stress. D/E of 205.5% signals heavy leverage with $2.1B total debt against a $512M market cap [4]. ROE is deeply negative at -117% due to massive goodwill impairment charges driving a -137.5% profit margin [4]. Revenue declined -2.6% YoY to $534M in Q1 2026, with management acknowledging 'poor commercial execution over the last two years' that will take several quarters to resolve [1]. The 3-year revenue CAGR of ~0.8% (2022-2025) reflects stagnation. The sole bright spot is a Forward P/E of 3.19x — extraordinarily cheap if the $0.29 forward EPS materializes [4]. Free cash flow grew 20% YoY to $53M in Q1 2026, providing some near-term liquidity cushion [1]. Liquidity floor cleared: 35M avg volume x $0.94 = $32.7M daily [4].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (4/9): Alight's core business — benefits administration, healthcare navigation, and HR services — faces meaningful AI disruption risk. Large language models and AI agents from competitors like ADP, Workday, and new entrants could replicate Alight's benefits guidance and employee support functions at lower cost [2]. CEO Rohit Verma acknowledged the company must organize data into a 'knowledge layer' to compete, with $100M+ planned investment in 2026 not expected to yield efficiencies until 2027 [2]. Analysts at BeyondSPX flagged Alight's 'execution crisis meets AI ambition,' noting the company is a niche player competing against scaled giants with deeper AI resources [2]. The services-heavy model with human call centers is particularly vulnerable to AI-driven automation. AI Opportunity (1/1): Alight LumenAI is shipping to customers via the Worklife platform, and the AI-powered Alight Assistant delivers personalized benefits guidance using employer-specific data. Fortune recognized Alight as one of America's Most Innovative Companies in 2026, suggesting early AI monetization traction [2].

infinite_game.summary: Alight exhibits finite game characteristics — frequent CEO turnover (three CEOs in recent years), strategy driven by financial distress rather than purpose, and commercial execution failures suggesting short-term optimization over long-term capability building. New CEO Rohit Verma brings a turnaround mandate focused on AI investment, but the company has yet to demonstrate the organizational stability and purpose-driven leadership that characterize infinite game players.

just_cause.analysis: Alight's purpose 'Be a beacon. Empower others. Be Alight.' exists but reads as generic corporate positioning rather than a Just Cause that drives strategic decisions. Frequent leadership changes, strategic pivots driven by financial necessity, and commercial execution failures suggest the cause doesn't shape behavior. No evidence leadership references the purpose when making difficult trade-offs [3]. Score: 1 weighted point.

courage_to_lead.analysis: New CEO Rohit Verma committed $100M+ to building an AI 'knowledge layer' in 2026 despite revenue declining and the stock trading near all-time lows — a bet on long-term capability over short-term earnings [2]. The 2022 divestiture of payroll and professional services to focus on cloud-based benefits was a strategic decision to sacrifice near-term revenue for positioning, though arguably forced by competitive dynamics. Score: 1 weighted point.

trusting_teams.analysis: Glassdoor reviews describe invisible C-suite leadership, limited growth opportunities, inconsistent communication, and rare compensation increases [2]. Executive team rated 'C-' on Comparably (bottom 30%). Three CEOs in recent years — Dave Guilmette replaced by Rohit Verma in January 2026 — signals organizational instability. A 'major C-suite shakeup' with new technology and delivery chiefs further disrupts team continuity [2]. Score: 0 weighted points.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Alight divested its payroll business to focus on cloud-based benefits and is now pivoting toward AI-enabled service delivery with LumenAI. These are meaningful strategic shifts but not transformative reinventions — the company remains in benefits administration, using technology to improve delivery rather than fundamentally redefining its business model [2]. Score: 1 weighted point.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Alight's competitive framing acknowledges rivals' strengths — management positions against 'scaled giants like ADP and Workday' and competes on specialization rather than breadth. CEO Verma's emphasis on building unique employer-specific data assets rather than matching competitors' scale suggests a mindset focused on improving distinctive capabilities rather than 'beating' competitors [2]. Score: 1 weighted point.

investment_thesis: Alight is a deeply distressed turnaround play trading at $0.94 — down 85% from its 52-week high of $6.11 — with a Forward P/E of just 3.2x if the $0.29 forward EPS materializes [4]. Q1 2026 revenue of $534M beat estimates by 6.2%, free cash flow grew 20% YoY to $53M, and the company still serves ~70% of Fortune 100 employers with sticky benefits administration contracts [1]. New CEO Rohit Verma's $100M+ AI investment in a 'knowledge layer' built on proprietary employer-specific data could differentiate Alight from generic AI solutions, and Fortune named the company one of America's Most Innovative Companies in 2026 [2]. At $512M market cap for a company generating $140M in annual free cash flow, the valuation implies near-zero optionality on the AI pivot succeeding.
thesis_continued: The bear case is severe: $2.1B in debt against a $512M market cap (4x leverage), GAAP profit margins of -137.5% due to goodwill impairment, ROE of -117%, and revenue declining for two consecutive years due to what management calls 'poor commercial execution' [1][4]. Benefits administration faces structural AI disruption as LLMs can replicate employee guidance at lower cost, and Alight competes against vastly better-capitalized competitors — ADP ($120B market cap), Workday ($70B) — with deeper AI resources and R&D budgets [2]. Leadership instability — three CEOs in recent years, a C-suite shakeup in 2026, and Glassdoor reviews describing invisible leadership and a 'C-' executive team rating — undermines execution confidence at a critical moment. Q2 2026 revenue guidance of $490-505M implies continued sequential decline, and analysts forecast an 8% full-year revenue drop [1]. The penny-stock status ($0.94) creates additional institutional ownership and financing risks.
key_catalysts: Q1 2026 revenue beat of 6.2% above estimates with FCF up 20% YoY to $53M suggests operational stabilization under new CEO Verma, with Q2 guidance of $490-505M as the next test of turnaround trajectory [1]

The $100M+ AI 'knowledge layer' investment leveraging proprietary employer-specific benefits data could create defensible AI capabilities that generic competitors cannot replicate, with Fortune's 2026 Most Innovative Companies recognition validating early traction [2]

At 3.2x forward P/E and $512M market cap against $140M annual free cash flow, any stabilization in revenue and proof of the AI pivot could drive significant multiple expansion from deeply distressed levels [4]
key_risks: Crushing debt burden of $2.1B against $512M market cap creates existential risk if the turnaround stalls — management's admission of 'poor commercial execution over the last two years' shows revenue decline is self-inflicted, not cyclical [1][4]

Benefits administration faces structural AI disruption as LLMs replicate employee guidance at lower cost, and Alight competes as a niche player against ADP ($120B) and Workday ($70B) with vastly superior AI resources and R&D budgets [2]

Leadership instability with three CEOs in recent years, C-suite shakeup in 2026, and Glassdoor 'C-' executive team rating undermines execution credibility for a company that needs flawless turnaround execution [2]
